summary: EXP-287 closes WIN-017.c3 hole #3 (cross-validation circularity preemption — c2 carry-forward) with a single sentence inserted into WIN-017.detail_evidence between the existing "all methods agree" cross-validation clause and the "Spacecraft-based measurements" sentence. The inserted sentence flags spectroscopic-class distance estimates as the independence axis that does not flow through period-luminosity calibration of nearby parallaxes. Patch is detail_evidence-only; no other field on WIN-017 changes; no other WIN changes; no data values, counts, or classifications affected. Replacement detail_evidence is provided in EXP-287.json under replacement_detail_evidence (and equivalently as an insertion at insertion_anchor_before). Kernel-of-truth acknowledged: the dome defender is genuinely correct that Cepheid + RR Lyrae PL relations are calibrated using parallaxes of nearby standards (most prominently Riess et al. Gaia DR3 zero-point work), so "all methods agree" overstated the cleanness of cross-check for those two methods specifically. The fix preserves the original argument while removing the legitimate piece of the dome-defender objection. Three anticipated objections answered in the EXP file. Decider can close ISS-1847 with this patch.
